heading: Military spouse retirement plan eligibility credit for small employers
label: 45AA Military spouse retirement plan eligibility credit for small employers
notes: Effective DateSection applicable to taxable years beginning after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2022 Amendment note under .Dec. 29, 2022section 112(e) of Pub. L. 117–328section 38 of this title
amendments: Effective DateSection applicable to taxable years beginning after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2022 Amendment note under .Dec. 29, 2022section 112(e) of Pub. L. 117–328section 38 of this title

children.text: $200 with respect to each military spouse who is an employee of such employer and who participates in an eligible defined contribution plan of such employer at any time during such taxable year, plus

so much of the contributions made by such employer (other than an elective deferral (as defined in section 402(g)(3)) to all such plans with respect to such employee during such taxable year as do not exceed $300.1So in original. Probably should be followed by another closing parenthesis.1

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: b Limitation
children.text: An individual shall only be taken into account as a military spouse under subsection (a) for the taxable year which includes the date on which such individual began participating in the eligible defined contribution plan of the employer and the 2 succeeding taxable years.

children.heading: Eligible small employer
children.label: c Eligible small employer
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “eligible small employer” means an eligible employer (as defined in section 408(p)(2)(C)(i)(I).2So in original. Another closing parenthesis probably should precede the period.2

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “military spouse” means, with respect to any employer, any individual who is married (within the meaning of section 7703 as of the first date that the employee is employed by the employer) to an individual who is a member of the uniformed services (as defined , United States Code) serving on active duty. For purposes of this section, an employer may rely on an employee’s certification that such employee’s spouse is a member of the uniformed services if such certification provides the name, rank, and service branch of such spouse.section 101(a)(5) of title 10

children.heading: Exclusion of highly compensated employees
children.label: 2 Exclusion of highly compensated employees
children.text: With respect to any employer, the term “military spouse” shall not include any individual if such individual is a highly compensated employee of such employer (within the meaning of section 414(q)).

military spouses employed by such employer are eligible to participate in such plan not later than the date which is 2 months after the date on which such individual begins employment with such employer, and

children.label: A
children.text: are immediately eligible to receive an amount of employer contributions under such plan which is not less the amount of such contributions that a similarly situated participant who is not a military spouse would be eligible to receive under such plan after 2 years of service, and

children.label: B
children.text: immediately have a nonforfeitable right to the employee’s accrued benefit derived from employer contributions under such plan.

children.heading: Aggregation rule
children.label: f Aggregation rule
children.text: All persons treated as a single employer under subsection (b), (c), (m), or () of section 414 shall be treated as one employer for purposes of this section.o
